intro: Hieracium soldanoi, also known as Soldano's hawkweed, is a rare and endemic species of flowering plant in the dandelion-like genus Hieracium. The plant has been reported to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential remedy for wound healing and infection prevention. The bioactive constituents of Hieracium soldanoi include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in folk medicine to treat digestive issues and skin conditions, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
